title_en: Farm vs. Wild Salmon: The Color Truth
title_es: Salmón de Granja vs. Salvaje: La Verdad del Color
hook_en: Why is your salmon pink? Let's find out!
key_message: Farm-raised salmon is dyed pink using synthetic astaxanthin, while wild salmon get their color naturally.
visual_elements: Close-up of both types of salmon fillets side by side
Zoom in on a color chart used by salmon farms
Quick pan over a wild salmon swimming in clear water
_source_food_context:
DAILY STAPLE COMPARISON [Protein]:
• HEALTHY: Wild-caught salmon (sockeye, king, coho — naturally fed on krill and smaller fish)
• PROCESSED/CONVENTIONAL: Farm-raised salmon (crowded pens, antibiotics, artificial color added to feed — astaxanthin from petrochemicals)
• KEY FACT: Farm-raised salmon is dyed pink via synthetic astaxanthin in their feed — without it the flesh is gray. Wild salmon gets its color naturally from eating krill. Farm-raised also has higher PCB and dioxin levels
• VISUAL IDEA: Show a 'color chart' that salmon farms use to pick the exact shade of pink for their fish — 'they literally choose the color like paint swatches'

title_en: The Real Cost of Farmed Salmon
title_es: El Verdadero Costo del Salmón de Granja
hook_en: Farm-raised or Wild? Let's dive into the facts!
hook_es: ¿Salmón de granja o salvaje? ¡Vamos a los hechos!
key_message: Farm-raised salmon contains higher levels of PCBs and dioxins, unlike wild-caught salmon.
visual_elements: Split screen of farm-raised vs. wild salmon
Animated arrows pointing to 'PCBs' and 'Dioxins' on farmed salmon
Overhead shot of salmon farming facility
